item.title: Congo Eurobonds Defy Ebola as Investors Chase Rare Yield, AI Exposure
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo’s Eurobonds have remained resilient even as it grapples with an Ebola outbreak, buoyed by high yields and the nation’s exposure to metals that underpin the global artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Reliance Traders Said to Game Plan in Case RBI Raises Rates
item.description: Traders in Reliance Industries Ltd.’s treasury department are strategizing over where to park the company’s cash in case the Reserve Bank of India starts raising interest rates in the coming months.

item.title: Fuji Media’s Real Estate Unit Attracts 1 Trillion Yen Bids
item.description: Fuji Media Holdings Inc. has received stronger-than-expected interest in its real estate subsidiary, attracting bidders including KKR & Co., Blackstone Inc. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc., people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: AI Boom Propels China Optical Maker to Top Weighting on CSI 300
item.description: A Chinese optical transceiver maker has overtaken a battery giant as the top-weighted firm in China’s equities benchmark, the latest sign of how a broadening AI rally is reshaping the world’s second-largest stock market.

item.title: SoftBank’s PayPay to Buy T&D’s Life Insurer for $840 Million
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s payments unit is buying the life insurance unit of T&D Holdings Inc. for ¥134.3 billion ($840 million) to broaden its offerings and better compete in Japan’s ballooning fintech market.

item.title: BofA Says Foreign Buyers Lured by UK Yields Despite Politics
item.description: Bank of America predicts a streak of gilt purchases by foreign buyers extended through May, as some of the highest government yields in major developed nations offset political turbulence and inflation fears.

item.title: Foreigners Offload Japanese Stocks as AI Bubble Worries Grow
item.description: Overseas investors turned net sellers of Japanese shares for the first time in two months last week, ending a buying streak that helped drive the nation’s benchmark to multiple all-time highs.

item.title: Japan Officials Met Metals, LNG Firms in Unusual Moscow Talks
item.description: Japanese officials who visited Moscow last week met commodity exporters along with Russian government representatives in a rare high-level commercial contact between their countries since President Vladimir Putin ordered the 2022 full scale invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon on Running a Bank in the Age of AI
item.description: Why he doesn't see an AI jobs apocalypse coming to Wall Street.

item.title: Odd Lots: Goldman’s Solomon on Banks in the Age of AI (Podcast)
item.description: There’s a lot of debate about the future of AI — not just whether it will produce the returns investors are expecting, but also if AI will lead to mass worker displacement. Big banks are the perfect prism through which to explore some of these questions. Not only are they deploying AI very quickly, but they have a wide range of workers who are using the technology, from back-office employees to junior analysts to the most senior investment bankers. In this episode, we speak with David Solomon, c

item.title: Goldman Erects Lobby Rockets as Morgan Stanley IPO Rivalry Heats Up
item.description: No sooner had Goldman Sachs Group Inc. locked in top billing for the SpaceX IPO than Morgan Stanley dashed off a note to clients that it was actually co-lead on the deal, noting that their listing is in alphabetical order.

item.title: Ultra-Rich Africans Piling into Property to Preserve Wealth, Standard Bank Says
item.description: Africans with at least $50 million in investable assets are increasingly buying property for wealth preservation, diversification, and potential rental income, according to the continent’s biggest lender by assets.

item.title: Pirelli Denies Short Seller Report on Sales to Russia’s Military
item.description: Pirelli & Co. SpA denied a report from short seller Grizzly Research LLC that raised concerns about the Italian tire maker’s links to Russia and its military.

item.title: Czech Inflation Slows, Weakening Case for an Imminent Rate Hike
item.description: Czech inflation slowed more than expected, bolstering arguments against an imminent interest rate hike as policymakers gauge the Iran conflict’s broader impact on the economy.

item.title: UBS’s Khan Sees More Demand For Hedge Funds Than Private Credit
item.description: UBS Group AG said client sentiment remains positive but cautious despite geopolitical uncertainties and market turmoil, according to the bank’s co-presidents of global wealth management Iqbal Khan and Robert Karofsky.

item.title: Funds, Industry Buyers Eye BlackRock’s Australian Battery Builder Akaysha
item.description: Industry players and pension funds are among the suitors eyeing a stake of 50-60% in BlackRock-backed Australian battery developer Akaysha Energy Pty Ltd., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: MMC Port Is Said to Approach Bidders for Potential Stake Sale
item.description: Malaysian tycoon Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary’s MMC Port Holdings Sdn. has approached prospective bidders for a minority stake in the company, according to people familiar with the matter, after postponing an initial public offering last year.

item.title: Apollo Set to Hand Over Control of Reno de Medici to Bondholders
item.description: Apollo Global Management is set to hand over the keys of Reno de Medici SpA to its bondholders, as part of a debt restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Swiss Inflation Unexpectedly Holds Before SNB Rate Decision
item.description: Switzerland’s inflation rate came in lower in than economists expected last month, a sign the strength of the franc may be offsetting the impact of high energy costs.

item.title: Malaysian Palm Oil Exports to Face Pressure From Indonesian Push
item.description: Malaysian palm oil exports could tumble for a third straight month in June if buyers favor cheaper Indonesian supplies as Jakarta’s overhaul of commodity shipments sparks a push to move cargoes before the new rules fully take hold.

item.title: African Financier AFC Raises $2 Billion Fueled by Asian Banks
item.description: The Africa Finance Corp. raised $2 billion via a syndicated loan, with more than half provided by Asian and European banks seeking to capitalize on growing demand for infrastructure projects across the continent.

item.title: Sweden’s Subdued Inflation Gives Riksbank Rate Hike Reprieve
item.description: Sweden’s core inflation rate increased at a slower pace than the central bank had expected in May, giving it room to hold off on borrowing-cost increases in the wake of the Iran war.

item.title: Osaka Bourse to Add More Single-Stock Options for Market Makers
item.description: Osaka Exchange Inc. will increase the number of single-stock options in its market-making program in another effort to boost derivative use in Japan, bourse officials said.

item.title: New Zealand Bond Auction Draws Record Demand After Issuance Cuts
item.description: Demand for New Zealand’s 2031 government bonds surged to a record at auction after the government unexpectedly cut its projected borrowing needs for the coming years.

item.title: Indian Owner of Swiss Gold Refiner Probed for $159 billion of Inflated Revenue
item.description: India’s markets regulator has alleged that the owner of Swiss gold refiner Valcambi SA overstated revenue, according to an interim order issued Wednesday.

item.title: Russia Spring Wheat Planting Continues to Lag on Poor Weather
item.description: Russian spring wheat planting continues to face delays as persistent rain hinders growers, risking lower harvests.

item.title: FTSE 100 Falls as Oil Shares Weigh
RBA Is Already Seeing Signs Rate Hikes Are Working, Bullock Says
item.description: Australia’s central bank has already seen some signs that its interest-rate increases are starting to work through the economy, Governor Michele Bullock said, reiterating that the monetary policy board will do what’s necessary to achieve its mandate.

item.title: Asia Steps Up Currency Defense as Korea, Indonesia Vow Action
item.description: Asian authorities are ramping up their currency defense as high energy costs and bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates pressure them.

item.title: Oil Rally Spurs Nigeria to Weigh Refinancing High-Cost Debt
item.description: Nigeria plans to refinance high-cost debt and raise funds to help plug its budget deficit, leveraging strong investor confidence amid elevated oil prices fueled by the US-Iran conflict.
